title: Calorimeter Energy Response Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Calorimeter Energy Response Inversion: Calorimeter calibration: determine energy response and resolution functions from test beam or in-situ data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.shower.em_hadronic…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; aging_degradation_percent, temperature_variation_effect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: E_meas = a * E_true + b + sigma_E * noise; EM linearity + hadronic e/h ratio
E.world_state_x: calibration coefficients a(eta, phi, E) and resolution parameters sigma_E/E
E.observation_y: measured cluster energies E_meas for single particles (test beam) or in-situ (Z->ee, pi0->gamma gamma)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered energy_calibration_coefficients is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); aging_degradation_percent dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Detector gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: energy resolution < 10%/sqrt(E) for EM, < 50%/sqrt(E) for hadronic. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |aging_degradation_percent|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_cells * N_events) for calibration coefficient determination per iteration
